Interactive questions require the local LearnLoop server.

LearnLoop Course

ACP Fundamentals

Learn Agent Client Protocol through small concepts, local questions, and agent-ready learning context.

Read one module at a time. When a concept gets fuzzy, ask directly beside that section. LearnLoop stores the question with its exact course context so an agent can answer and improve the material later.

01 What ACP is and why it existsUnderstand the client-agent integration problem ACP tries to solve. 02 Communication modelRead ACP as JSON-RPC over a transport, with clear client and agent roles. 03 Initialize and capabilitiesLearn the handshake and why capability negotiation matters. 04 Prompt, resources, and tool callsSee how prompts carry content, references, and tool requests. 05 ACP, MCP, and A2ASeparate the protocol layers so you choose the right one. 06 Build a minimal ACP clientSketch the smallest local client and the buffering rules that matter. 07 Bridge ACP to the browserUse a local bridge when a web UI needs to talk to a stdio agent. 08 Boundaries and judgmentDecide when ACP is worth the extra moving parts.
